title: azure-kv: HTTP 429 maps to Backend instead of Unavailable
description:
## Problem
is_transient() in secretx-azure-kv/src/lib.rs checks only is_server_error() (5xx). HTTP 429 Too Many Requests is a 4xx code so is_server_error() returns false. Result: Azure API rate-limit responses map to SecretError::Backend (permanent). Callers using retry-on-Unavailable never retry.

## Same bug pattern as doppler and vault, just fixed in ac9a1e4.

## Fix
In is_transient(), add the 429 check:
```rust
fn is_transient(e: &azure_core::Error) -> bool {
    matches!(e.kind(), ErrorKind::Io)
        || matches!(
            e.kind(),
            ErrorKind::HttpResponse { status, .. }
            if status.is_server_error() || *status == azure_core::http::StatusCode::TooManyRequests
        )
}
```
azure_core::http::StatusCode::TooManyRequests confirmed present in azure_core 0.35.0 (src/http/poller.rs:1050).

## Acceptance
Unit test: is_transient on a 429 error returns true. cargo test -p secretx-azure-kv passes.
